key_insight: Black phosphorus’s momentum-space quantum geometry can be linked to a measurable low-loss EELS signal through a restricted quantum weight that remains well behaved even when single-band quantum metrics become singular.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical bulk spectroscopic route to quantify integrated quantum geometry in anisotropic 2D materials, useful for guiding optoelectronic and quantum-device design.
method: Built a 32-band DFT-Wannier Hamiltonian for analytic momentum-derivative geometry calculations.
Computed single-band and occupied-manifold quantum metric / quantum weight maps and enforced restricted Souza-Wilkens-Martin sum rules.
Excluded zero-loss contributions to define an experimentally relevant restricted quantum weight for low-loss EELS.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports nearly isotropic in-plane restricted quantum weight, Kzz/Kxx = 0.972 ± 0.005, with only a small +0.46% per eV U drift under Hubbard-Hartree corrections.
watch_item: Raw single-band quantum metric maps can be misleading in the conventional cell because folding and near-degeneracies create genuine singularities, so masked or occupied-manifold quantities should be used.
paper_title: Quantum weight and low-loss EELS signatures of Wannier quantum geometry in black phosphorus
